Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Hi,Jimmy,
   I think that the version should be that which is defined in POM.XML.For example: if we pull a Frankfurt branch and the version 1.5.0 is defined in the pom file, after the issue is modified, the OOM file will use version 1.5.0. Thanks.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> Tested the new ESR versions in Frankfurt environment without success.

I will need someone to fix the frankfurt version of these components.  I reported early in the Frankfurt release that there was not support for a new version this release and we were going to use the elalto 1.4 versions. Then integration raised the flag on root containers, and said that we had to have it, so I tried to accomodate it.  @Peng, can you fix the frankfurt branch?  And 1.5.1 is the proper container ID, I had to upversion it.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> When I take a look at the logs, I'm not sure it's really working...
> https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//106960-1/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/pod-onap-esr-server-5db84f4df6-dbw87-esr-server-logs.html

Judging from the return of the error message, the error should be returned by the AAI, that is, the call has reached the AAI through the MSB, and the AAI prompts that the access failed: "Node Not Found: No Node of type cloud-region found at: cloud-infrastructure / cloud- regions "

Do, do we need a new container?  I am still unclear what our path forward is.  As I understand it - I released 1.5.1 of esr-gui and 1.5.3 or esr-server.  According to Andreas, a test case that works with 1.4.0 of esr-gui does not work with 1.5.1. Are there pending changes in a 1.5.2 container that can be released that will fix that issue?
